title: Bridge Essay: Peripheral immune cells and perinatal brain injury: a double-edged sword?

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through Peripheral, immune, cells, and, perinatal.

**Peripheral immune cells and perinatal brain injury: adouble-edged sword?** — Pediatr Res. PMID: 34750522. DOI: 10.1038/s41390-021-01818-7. Perinatal brain injury is the leading cause of neurological mortality and morbidity in childhood ranging from motor and cognitive impairment to behavioural and neuropsychiatric disorders. Various noxious stimuli, including perinatal inflammation, chronic and acute hypoxia, hyperoxia, stress and drug exposure contribute to the pathogenesis.
